content:
I recently discovered Google Summer of Code (GSoC), a prestigious and globally recognised program that provides students with the opportunity to contribute to open-source software. Beyond the attractive stipend, thousands of students participate each year to gain invaluable industry experience and build a “job-ready” portfolio.

1 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Choosing My Path: The Drupal Association
After researching several participating organisations, I decided to contribute to the Drupal Association. It is a highly reputable community, and the scale of their impact on the web is truly inspiring.
Facing the Learning Curve
However, my choice came with an immediate challenge. While I have built a strong foundation in HTML, CSS, Tailwind CSS, JavaScript, React and TypeScript, Drupal is primarily built on a PHP-based architecture.
Instead of letting this deter me, I’ve decided to embrace the learning curve.
First Steps: Setting Up and Starting Small
Today marks the official beginning of my PHP journey. To get started, I installed XAMPP to set up a local development environment. I spent my first session diving into the fundamentals: Variables and Data Types in PHP.
It’s only Day 1, but I’m excited to bridge the gap between my front-end skills and the world of PHP development.
